visual_features.overall_style: Very minimalist, content-first, with a corporate/utility feel. White background, light gray dividers, black/gray typography, and almost no decorative elements. It feels like a straightforward WordPress-style theme adapted for livestock auction listings.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is established primarily with font size/weight and left alignment. The page title 'Auction Lot 7' is the largest, boldest text and anchors the main column. Below it, body content uses smaller but still bold section labels (e.g., 'Attention Buyers & Sellers:', 'February 11, 2026') followed by normal-weight details. In the sidebar, the 'Auction Lots' heading is slightly larger and bolder than the list items, differentiating section title from navigation links. Navigation in the header is kept low-contrast and small to not compete with the main title.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderately spacious with comfortable margins around the main content column and padding inside the header. There is significant blank space above the main headline and between the main content and the right sidebar, especially emphasized by a vertical divider line. Line spacing is generous, and list items in the sidebar have enough vertical padding to be distinct but not overly spread out.
unique_elements: Two-column layout with a subtle vertical divider line between main content and sidebar
Very text-heavy layout with no content imagery on the detail page, keeping focus on auction information
Right sidebar acting as a contextual index of all auction lots, resembling a secondary navigation list
Header structure with centered primary navigation and a search icon aligned to the far right

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit two-column grid likely based on a 12-column system, with main content occupying approximately 8–9 columns and the right sidebar 3–4 columns.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic blog-style layout with full-width header, then a main content column on the left and a vertical sidebar on the right.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Header at top with logo, nav, and search icon. Below that, a main content section containing the auction lot title and a vertical stack of textual information. To the right, a vertical sidebar section containing a search field and a text-based list of auction lot links.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Strong left alignment for both the main content and sidebar content. Header nav items are horizontally centered between logo and search icon, with consistent baseline alignment. Text is left-aligned across the page, reinforcing a linear reading experience.
responsive_hints: Sidebar is visually separate and narrower, indicating that on smaller screens it will likely stack below the main content.
Horizontal navigation with widely spaced items suggests a breakpoint where the menu collapses into a mobile menu or icon.
Generous horizontal padding within the main content column implies that padding will reduce at narrower widths to preserve line length.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. The page starts with a slim header bar (logo left, navigation center, search icon right). Beneath the header, the content splits into two columns: on the left, the auction lot title followed by a vertically stacked set of paragraphs/label-value lines describing dates, seller, head count, weights, origin, and description; on the right, a sidebar with a small search field at the top and a vertically stacked text list of auction lot links below. No alternating columns or visual modules—just a straightforward column + sidebar stack.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header consumes a small vertical band across the full width. The primary content area below occupies the majority of the viewport height, with the left column taking roughly 65–70% width and the right sidebar 30–35%. Within the left column, the title and first paragraphs sit close to the top margin, and subsequent text fills most of the vertical space. The sidebar search field takes a small block at the top, while the 'Auction Lots' heading and list of links occupy the remainder, with ample top margin above the heading.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately low density. Although the page is text-heavy, line spacing and paragraph spacing keep it readable. The sidebar links are single-line items with modest separation, leading to a clean but not sparse list. Overall, the interface is more functional than decorative, but not cramped.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Reading flow is a strong left-to-right, then top-to-bottom pattern. The eye lands first on the page title ‘Auction Lot 7’, then moves down through the sequential text blocks describing auction details. The vertical divider and whitespace then pull the eye to the right sidebar, where the user can scan the 'Auction Lots' list top-to-bottom. In the header, the eye moves from the logo on the far left, across the navigation links, and finally to the search icon on the far right, forming a soft Z-pattern at the very top of the page.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons (e.g., the 'Search' button in the sidebar search field) are simple rectangular shapes with slightly rounded corners (~3–4px radius). They use a light gray fill (#D0D0D0) with darker gray text. Borders are either subtle or same color as the background, maintaining a flat, minimal aesthetic. No visible drop shadow; design leans flat rather than skeuomorphic. Padding is modest, giving a compact but clickable target.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typically implemented as either a <button> element or an <input type="submit"> adjacent to an <input type="search"> inside a wrapping form. Often grouped in a flex or inline-block container so that the input and button appear as a single control with aligned heights.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit card components are visible; content areas behave like implicit cards via layout rather than box styling. Where card-like regions exist (main content column vs sidebar), they are separated by a vertical divider (#E5E5E5) and internal padding rather than shadows or borders. Corners are sharp (0–2px radius), giving a very flat editorial look.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: When cards are needed, they would likely be plain <div> containers with padding and maybe a top-level heading and body copy. Structure would be title (h2/h3) followed by paragraphs or lists. Separation from other cards would be via margin rather than thick borders or shadows.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Form elements are minimal: the visible search field in the sidebar is a rectangular input with a white background, light gray border, and square or slightly rounded corners. The label is not visible; instead, a placeholder or the adjacent 'Search' button clarifies purpose. No strong focus styling shown, but typical behavior would be a slightly darker border on focus. Spacing is compact, with the button tightly aligned to the input to look like a compound control.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms use stacked layout: a single row containing the search input and button inside a <form> element. Labels may be implicit (aria-label, title) or positioned above inputs in other parts of the site. Inputs likely span full sidebar width minus padding, implemented using block-level inputs styled via CSS.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal text menu in the header. Each nav item is simple text with no borders or backgrounds, likely using medium gray (#555555) with slightly increased letter-spacing. Items are evenly spaced with generous horizontal padding/margins. The active item may be distinguished via darker color or a subtle underline (not clearly visible in the screenshot). The overall style is flat and unobtrusive, supporting the logo and content rather than competing with it.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Most likely built as a semantic <nav> containing an unordered list (<ul><li><a>...), styled inline via flexbox or inline-block to display horizontally. Active state is controlled by CSS classes on <li> or <a>. Secondary navigation (the 'Auction Lots' list) in the sidebar behaves similarly but vertically stacked, functioning as contextual navigation links.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The sidebar 'Auction Lots' is a vertical text list of links. Each item is a single line of text with minimal vertical spacing (around 4–8px gap). No bullets or icons; the differentiation comes from link color and underline on hover. There are no visible separators (lines) between items, resulting in a clean, text-focused list. Visual hierarchy: section title 'Auction Lots' (slightly larger/bolder) followed by uniform-weight list items.

heading.visual_style: Headings appear as a neutral, modern sans-serif with moderate weight and clean geometry—functional rather than decorative, similar to a grotesque sans with good legibility.

body.visual_style: Body text is a clean, humanist/grotesque sans-serif with good readability and a balanced x-height, optimized for paragraphs of plain text.

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical component padding is 16px–24px on all sides for main content areas and 12px–16px within sidebar widgets and inputs.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., header and content, content and footer or sidebar blocks) is around 32px–48px, creating clear breaks without feeling sparse.

interaction_hints: Text links in navigation and sidebar likely change color and/or show an underline on hover, with no heavy animations.
Buttons such as the 'Search' button probably darken slightly on hover and may show a subtle border color change to indicate interactivity.
Form inputs likely show a darker border or glow when focused, following standard browser behavior with minimal custom styling.
Transitions, if used, are likely very subtle (150–200ms color fades) to preserve the utilitarian, content-first feel.
List items in the sidebar may use pointer cursor and slight color change on hover to reinforce clickability without additional chrome.
